prompt:
Third time this quarter someone's hand-rolled Set-Cookie string builder has shipped a broken attribute, and our own header parsing is a `split('=')` that mangles values containing an equals sign. I want a single wire-format cookie module for the gateway: cookies.ts, TypeScript, node stdlib only, pure functions. This is NOT a session store — it only speaks the header formats.

A cookie object is { name, value, domain?, path?, expires? (epoch ms), maxAge? (seconds, integer, negatives legal), secure, httpOnly (booleans), sameSite? ('Strict' | 'Lax' | 'None') }.

Exports:

1. parseCookieHeader(header) — the request-side Cookie header → Record<string,string>. Split on ';', trim around each pair AND around the first '='; everything after the first '=' belongs to the value; a surrounding pair of double quotes is stripped from the value. Duplicate names: FIRST wins. Segments with no '=', empty segments, and empty names are skipped. Absolutely no percent-decoding — callers own their encoding.

2. parseSetCookie(line) — one response-side Set-Cookie line → cookie object or null. Attribute names are case-insensitive; unknown attributes are ignored; when an attribute repeats the LAST one wins (that's the storage model, opposite of the Cookie header rule, don't mix them up). Domain: strip one leading dot, lowercase. Expires: parse the HTTP date, unparseable → attribute dropped. Max-Age: optional minus then digits only, else dropped. SameSite: case-insensitive, normalized to Strict/Lax/None, unknown values dropped. Return null (reject the whole cookie, like a browser would) when: there's no name=value pair at all or the name is empty; SameSite=None without Secure; a __Secure- name without Secure; a __Host- name without Secure, without an explicit Path=/, or with any Domain.

3. serializeSetCookie(cookie) → the Set-Cookie string, attributes in exactly this order: name=value; Domain=; Path=; Expires=; Max-Age=; Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=. Expires renders as an IMF-fixdate ('Wed, 09 Jun 2027 10:18:14 GMT'). Domain is lowercased with a leading dot stripped. Throw TypeError on anything we'd regret emitting: a name that isn't an RFC 2616 token (empty, spaces, ';', ',', '='...), a value containing whitespace, ';', ',', double quote, or backslash (empty value is fine), a path that doesn't start with '/' or contains ';', a non-integer maxAge, an unknown sameSite, SameSite None without secure, and the same __Secure-/__Host- prefix rules as parsing.

4. cookieExpiresAt(cookie, nowMs) → epoch ms, or null for a session cookie (neither Expires nor Max-Age). Max-Age BEATS Expires when both are present: nowMs + maxAge*1000. Zero or negative Max-Age means already expired.

5. isExpired(cookie, nowMs) → true when cookieExpiresAt is non-null and <= nowMs. No Date.now() anywhere in the module — the clock always arrives as an argument.

Acceptance is node --test test_cookies.ts. The test file is read-only.
